channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: >tfw fell for the NEET meme how the fuck can you guys endure this? — 48 replies — 192.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>tfw fell for the NEET meme how the fuck can you guys endure this?

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:47 UTC

OP

>tfw fell for the NEET meme
how the fuck can you guys endure this? it's so boring and depressing, I unironically miss my wagecuck 9-5

Early replies (first 3)

62156768
I would unironically wagecuck still if I was able to take vacation whenever I want. I don't enjoyed scheduling things like that or requiring permission.

62156775
>>62156751
I became a neet because of crippling social anxiety, not out of choice.
I suspect many are the same.

62156779
>>62156751
see you fell for it without having any money, I did that once and then realized that it costs money to do anything other than sit in front of a screen all day every day

Depending on where you live, even going out for a walk will cost you bus fare or gas to get to a decent park. Never felt so trapped, now if I had money to go out and do things like camping or fishing or even just cook nice meals then I would have been a lot happier

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156905
>>62156794
are you me? that's what I'm doing right now
I had 65K last October
Now down to 35K savings
Applied to 400 jobs
it's gonna turn around any minute now
finance / accounting Excel guru by the way I'll run your entire accounting and finance
MBA no CPA hire me please
>no homo

62156906
>>62156751
You were buckbroken, anon. Elites don't "miss" work kek. Only buckbroken wagebrains do.

62156917
>>62156751
“Let's suppose that you were able every night to dream any dream that you wanted to dream. And that you could, for example, have the power within one night to dream 75 years of time. Or any length of time you wanted to have. And you would, naturally as you began on this adventure of dreams, you would fulfill all your wishes. You would have every kind of pleasure you could conceive. And after several nights of 75 years of total pleasure each, you would say "Well, that was pretty great." But now let's have a surprise. Let's have a dream which isn't under control. Where something is gonna happen to me that I don't know what it's going to be. And you would dig that and come out of that and say "Wow, that was a close shave, wasn't it?" And then you would get more and more adventurous, and you would make further and further out gambles as to what you would dream. And finally, you would dream ... where you are now. You would dream the dream of living the life that you are actually living today.”

62157054
>>62156887
>>62156905
See if ULINE has anything that'll work for you. Most people forget they even exist.

62157069
>>62156906
This.
I copefarm reddit and all the working class love the feeling of being between jobs. They say they feel so free and it is a humiliating handing out resumes and going to interviews.

62157072
>>62156917
Kys Alan watts

62157101
>Everyday I lie awake at night and ask myself..what if this is it? What if I don't make it?

62157117
>>62156838
wish i could study like i were back in college again, i have forgotten everything. once i earned an A in calculus, i was so sharp, and i swore that i would keep it going. that was thirty years ago.

62157126
>>62156779
Who the fuck is too poor to afford a bus what is this larp. If you can't ride a bus how do you have a roof over your head

62157134
>>62156917
>if you eat great food every day, eventually you'll be so bored you'll start eating shit
>if you sit on a comfy chair every day, eventually you'll be so bored you'll start sitting with a knife up your ass
I don't think that's how boredom works

62157204
>>62157117
I hear you. I don’t have the time nor the energy like I did back then; waging just drains a man for a pittance.
>>62156917
I fucked up so many times. It would be nice to wake up and it all fades away.

62157215
>>62157134
lmao!
Thats pretty much what wagies say
I just told some guy
>i am 35 and I never had a job
You know what he said with a sneer on his face?
>thats not the flex you think it is
LOL

62157256
>>62156905
>>62156794
how do you guys feel about AI

62157293
>>62157256
I WISH I HAD HELD ON TO MY MICRON LETF AT 50 BUCKS IN DECEMBER

NOW THAT IT'S 250 BUCKS

62157298
>>62157134
>>62157215
to be fair if you gave me a million bucks/financial security I would 'wagie' by trying to become a corporate finance lawyer, because I would want to have 10 million and 100 million and be a CEO. Because I can't stand being a broke bitch. Give me 70 years so I can become charlie munger or warren buffet. Fuck this poorfag shit.

62157475
>>62157298
None of the two people you mentioned came from rags, bro. Lol.

62157483
>>62157475
Yes and Trump got a million dollar loan

But even if he lost the loan his dad was worth half a billion anyway so he had no real risk. My point is if you give me financial security so that I don't feel like putting 100k into the market could bankrupt me or make me, I would be so much more than I am. But the system actively tries to fuck you in the ass.

Like playing as Wallachia in Victoria 3, you spend the entire game trying to absorb Moldova and liberate yourself and get great powers to help you fuck up Austria to get your wider territory back. By the time you're a great power it's already 1900 and the game is almost over.

62157525
>>62157483
2.5% of Americans have 1m

62157542
>>62157126
Most NEETs live really tight. They act smug about it here but they need to budget everything. I never fully could go NEETing because here and there I always did some tutoring to have some money for shit beyond food. I also missed out all the possible moonshots from 2012 onwards, including triple digit BTC and double digit ETH, single digit AMD, etc. because I never had money. In hindsight I could have skipped a computer upgrade back in 2019 but still it wouldn't made me a millionaire.
Dating also becomes impossible after 27. Girls start asking what you do for a living, and answering "I'm a college student" and "I live with my parents" is no longer good for them.
>>62156871
What hobbies can someone have when living with parents that set the rules in a small house? Painting? Writing? Cycling?

62157561
>>62157542
> and "I live with my parents" is no longer good for them.
If they know you don’t come from a rich family, yeah.

62157574
>>62157542
>Most NEETs live really tight. They act smug about it here but they need to budget everything
Who doesn’t have a budget? Rich and poors have a budget. Hell, it is probably only the rich that even have a budget.

62157589
>>62157525
>>62157542
AMD will probably run higher

But in just the last 8 months if you bought tech shit like Sandisk you could have made it, if you bought with 100k.

62157602
It's a quiet life. Being a neet in your teens/20s is fucked up, you're never gonna look better or have more energy.

But after that, if you didn't make it, why not? If you have the means to live without working. Dealing with bosses, with coworkers, traffic... sorry but nope.

62157704
>>62157542
>Girls start asking what you do for a living
i work in finance, managing a private pool of equity, with a focus on bank-to-bank transfer agents that facilitate trillions a year aka pools closed

62157756
>>62156751
>how the fuck can you guys endure this
They don't, they hate themselves.

62157769
>been neet since 2020
The biggest thing I have faced is what do you do when you have nothing to cope with? Most people's lives involve working and doing something to blow off steam after work. So it becomes a stress/release cycle. The boredom that comes is from doing all the release without the stress part. eventually your brain gets bored, and you don't really know who you are outside of that cycle.

Other consequences are that you don't realize how much structure a job really provides you. Sleep schedule, social outlets, and sense of belonging, to name a few. You really have to have your shit together because once that structure is gone, you're only left with yourself.

Some pro's are that you can really slow down to learn yourself. Take a full week trying something to see if it's for you. I've gone to college just to be a frat boy, went to a lineman school to see blue collar life, and worked in a bank to see white collar. More experiences than most, and my idea of the ideal life for me has formed more out of these experiences, without the pressure of needing to succeed in those fields. Which strangely makes me perform better "detachment" wink wink.

>become a master of finding and routing out your negative self-defeating talk
This is another pro, you get a lot of time to work on, and I believe Jesus when he said the kingdom of heaven is within you. When you can sit quietly with your mind, you can view your patterns clearly over a long period of time, and with the help of a therapist, really root out the major mental blocks.

You need to be selfish and learn yourself (slay your dragon) before you start your real life. Many people I see are so eager to pursue their career, family, and goals without knowing why they even want them.

62157987
>>62156751
Did this, regretted it. My advice is if you're gonna beer then have a social life, healthy diet and ample amounts of sunlight. Also learn something. If you don't want to be a wagie then ok but isolating yourself leads to mental and psychological issues.

62158000
>>62157215
I met a dude who was past 50 and he never had a job before. I asked him for advice lol.

62158001
>>62156751
just get fat, unironically
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jtwIERgy2OE

62158010
>>62156838
The majority of people is born to be cattle. They do not possess any internal motivation.

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 64 replies — 158.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:24 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62148936
Never tell anyone how much money you have. Poor, rich, anything.

62148947
>>62141874
What makes you think that?

62149370
>>62146661
Like >>62147680 it's was around $1000

62149399
>>62142226
Did you enjoy losing 3 mil, fucker?

62149941
>>62138453
Only my brother knows I bought BTC, we both stake together now as well, with native staking gaining traction. Imagine if I told my parents to buy at 120K Kek.

62150719
>>62142197
You never know

62150903
>>62148933
Still it has risks

62151073
>>62146671
I get that, but it’s better to check first before saying that. So far, it fits my standards. I don’t think firms like Ledger or even A16z would back it otherwise.

62151201
>>62148936
Greedy bastard

62151260
>>62151073
it's the same thing. Everything will fail in the end. Nothing survive

62151310
NEVER

62151368
>>62149941
Heartaches

62151461
>>62138453
How do we confess them?

62151818
>>62149941
Nice are you also waiting for the TBV launch? More interesting for me since it could unlock more use cases.

62152719
>>62142226
That's the goal lol

62152732
never ever tell anyone you own any crypto holy shit that is like rule #1 in life don't tell me you idiots are trying to orange pill your fucking friends and family

62152763
>>62138453
Why would you ever brag about your money? Especially when every cryptobro is a scrawny geek. Do you want to get tortured by niggers for your private key?

Secret wealthy is better

62153574
>>62150903
Every system has risk. The difference is just what kind of risk you’re exposed to and how much control you still keep over your assets.

62153589
>>62152732
Maybe it depends on who you’re closest to in your family. There’s usually one person you really vibe with.

62153929
>>62152763
Do what you wanna do

62154078
>>62142464
This here is the right play. Don't tell anyone how much money you have or flaunt it. It should be used to give you freedom not ego validation. Also when you reveal wealth to people they will try to use you and get some for themselves. Donate to charity and volunteer also though of course.

62154092
If you talk about bitcoin, people either assume you’re a sucker, a drug dealer, evading taxation, or a pedophile. I think what really limits its reach is that it’s technology, economics and political philosophy wrapped into an asset. Never selling.

62155160
>>62142464
Well...she's still your gf so do that...

62155300
>>62154092
Think the opposite

62155673
>>62155300
What’s the opposite?

62155774
I haven't told anyone how much I have invested, I just tell them my various investment schemes so they hopefully do something.
It's especially important for the kids because their zog parents tell them not to invest and to get a car for MUH FREEDOMS.

62155914
>>62151310
Ok, that's not a problem

62156005
Guys check out my crypto currency called hsgold I’m going to add a fair bit of liquidity at the end of the month when I get paid check it out highseasgold currently swapable for sol

62156014
>>62138453
That's RULE NUMBER ONE of the Ten Crack Commandments anon.

62157978
>>62138453
Don't do it

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 56 replies — 150.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:26 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62145007
>>62145001
>>62144995
>generally pretty quick to reply
The irony. Also you have to be 18+ to post here

62145009
>>62144501
>the meaning to this life is uhhhh if you're a good boy and follow this book, you'll have an infinitely long life after this one, 100% guaranteed, and in that life everything will be great, okay?

62145415
>>62143054
>>nofap
Difficult to do, confers immediate health and psychological benefits, and every normie has a bizarre level hatred for it.

That should tell you something.

62145428
>>62144238
Of course stoicism is a cuck mentality. It's a philosophy of decadence. No Greek during the peak of their civilization would have created something like stoicism the same way no boomber would have been interested in the meditations by marcus aurelius.

When the real world becomes too painful, many people retire and create ways of coping.

62145464
>>62145428
Real stoicism is a an "emptying"/devotion of yourself to higher Being. This is a living, communicative path that taps you into energetic circuits and correspondence. Stoicism as most people know it isn't transformative in any higher sense.

62145495
>>62145464
Well, I meant decadence in the societal sense. Individuals live in a society. Stoicism was born exactly when the greek dream ended and that's why it became so popular. Same way as Epicureism.

And that's why it is so goddamn popular now and you have depressed 21 yo kids talking about stoicism. It has become a meme. You're depressed? Read The Meditations and go to the gym, bro. The real world has become painful and people have to find ways to deal with the real world.

ps. By the way, Seneca moral writings are way more interesting than Marcus Aurelius' Meditations

62145579
>>62145009
Correct
>t. full trust that I'm where I need to be when I need to be there

62145590
>>62145579
retardmaxxing with extra steps

62145626
>>62145590
I wish I could impart to you even 1% of the things I've seen, alas just know its sort of like a Chinese finger trap you know what I mean?
>retardmaxxing
Okay, okay! Based!

62145653
>>62143054
i went all in on long distance ejaculation training, sucks to suck, peace i gotta catch a flight, there are some ceilings that need coating

62145752
>>62145005
rich dad poor dad taught me to put all my money in pokemon cards

62147432
Izzat farming thread
Abandon ship

62147439
It costs zero dollars to not be so darned rude

62147451
>>62143054

fuck i forgot about pomodoro. imagine having a faggy little tomato timer on your desk as an adult

62148909
>>62144289
this

62151619
>>62144395
... if you want to end up in hell

62151984
>>62144435
Marcus Aurelius didn't trust the slut, he knew she was bad when he married her. Thing was, she was the daughter of the previous emperor who was a cool guy and Aurelius married her out of loyalty to him (plus he got to be emperor himself out of it).

62152046
>>62143054
for me its several different stimulants and an ssri
amazingly im still a husk of a human, its honestly impressive financially speaking

62152662
Scam

62152741
>>62143054
Many of those things have improved my life a lot. Of course, I am not a billionaire but I like to enjoy the little things in life

62152905
>>62145005
You're supposed to read good fiction and let your mind wander, not that (((self help))) slop.

62155066
>>62143054
tbqh deepest post on this board

62155320
>>62147439
opportunity cost of missing a zinger tho

62156116
stupid demoralizing thread

62156188
>>62152741
>Many of those things have improved my life a lot
like what

62156224
>>62144272
>>62144395
>t.

62156315
>>62143054
My coming has gotten out of control that I don't even try to do nofap anymore. From 16-21 I was extremely passionate about but now at 27 I unironically have multiple screens of degeneracy on play and I even plan out my sessions.

62156324
>>62143054
Ask yourself this. Do any richfag elitists like Bezos do any of this?

62156689
>>62143054
This made me laugh so hard idk. I cant believe take things so seriously, it's sad

62157981
>>62156188
Improved my sperm count

item.title: Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the p… — 47 replies — 82.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the p…

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:15 UTC

OP

Sigh. So McDonald’s did it. They released the McValue® menu where each item is under $3. This new gimmick replaced the previous “Buy One, Add One for $1” menu AKA the only reason to have the app anymore after the deals turned to shit. I’ve also noticed with this update the drink prices increased by $0.20, a $0.40 increase YoY in my area. Yesterday I was starving after work but drove straight passed McDonald’s out of pure anger. Went home and ate some eggs and toast.

Thoughts?

Early replies (first 3)

62155716
>>62155708
Give it up, brother. Your fat ass doesn't need to be eating McDick's anyway.

62155723
>>62155708
For me, it's Wendy's. They still have a decent meal deal although it has gone up $1 over the past couple years. Ironically they have been a better value than Micky d's for a little while now.
>>62155716
Sounds like projection tb.h

62155728
>>62155708
learn to cook

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155851
>>62155748
To be fair, that's canadian dollars which are worth much less than usd

62155867
>>62155851
Canada and Australia should adopt a different dollar sign. They casually post prices in their shitty cheap plastic "dollars" on the internet like it's USD, and that confuses everyone.

62155874
>>62155708
>Yesterday I was starving after work but drove straight passed McDonald’s out of pure anger.
the goyim have gone insane

62155922
Anyone still eating McDonald’s can go get McFucked. I remember eating there as a kid in the 80s and they used to have events where some pedo used to larp as Ronald McDonald. The playground and food was great too. It was wholesome. The jews ruined that, along with everything else. Pizza Hut was also top tier. Fast food doesn’t deserve our money anymore.

62155943
>>62155708
"Product"

62155947
>>62155922

62155976
>>62155922
>Fast food doesn’t deserve our money anymore.
while the quality of ingredients at least outside the US has improved massively over the years, the overall price-to-value ratio has reached a point where you can get proper food at a restaurant for sometimes less but more often than not the same amount of money.
it just doesn't make sense anymore and too much of it is still bad for you so I guess I agree with you there.

62156013
McD is like the only cheap fast food place at this point.

$7.59 for a large meal. I just checked Wendys for a similar meal and they charge $11.59!

62156034
Will ozempic taken by every fatass in the country make mcdonalds drop their prices or will the influx of thinner people that think they can suddenly eat more make them raise?

62156037
>>62155708
It was $2.12 using the apps 40% off deal to get my one double cheeseburger because I didn't want two for $4, now it's $1.70. This works out better for me as long as they don't remove the app discount.

62156039
>>62155708
ill have 2 large fries no salt
4 mcdoubles extra onion extra ketchup add mayo
4 hot and spicy mcchickens extda mayo extra lettuce
40 mcnuggets all habanero ranch
big mac
uhhhhhhhhhhhh

62156052
>>62155737
>Top post on the FIRE subreddit is complaining about how much the price of taco bell has increased
If Taco Bell impacts your finances that much then you don't have enough to retire

62156090
>he didn’t take profits on the $5 meal deal
ngmi

62156160
>>62155732
Ok but why visit a real restaurant or at least some small business?

62156273
>>62155708
That fucking mcchicken used to be $1. Why is mcniggers still trying to call this shit a “value” menu.

62156289
>>62156273
yeah in 1980 dude. holy fuck for a finance subreddit this place sure does suck sometimes.
picrel is the menu around 1975.

62156291
>>62156273
honestly it should still be a buck, no way that sandwich costs more than 50 cents to make

62156292
>>62156289
the mcchicken was a dollar in the 2010's

62156294
>>62156292
sure, and rent was $500 and employees only made $4 per hour and insurance rates were cheap and franchising costs were cheap.

62156301
>>62156294
Why are you mad and wrong?
>>62156292
This poster is factually accurate. McD is crazy expensive now.

62156370
>>62155708
>crying that his goysyrup went up 20¢

62156472
>>62156160
Because I cannot be in two places at the same time. At least not in my corporeal form.

62156693
>>62155708
What happened to the DOLLAR MENU

Not the buy one and get one for a dollar I mean

THE DOLLAR MENU

62156721
>>62155716
I mean really. If the price of McDonald's is so high it bothers you then you're eating too much McDonald's, no way around it.

62156722
>>62155737
There has been a resurgence in smug girl posts with quality rebuttals

62156723
>>62155976
At this point it's a mouse trap

62156726
>>62155708
Fast food doesn't understand people eat their slop solely because its cheap. Probably for the better they price themselves out since people don't need to be eating that garbage anyways

62156731
These $5 meals are pathetic because I would need at least 2 of them to feel satisfied.

62156748
>>62155708
nothing on that menu should be over 99¢, and half of it should be less than 75¢

62157963
>>62155708
Those product looks gross

item.title: Once I get like a half a mill I'm just gonna buy and sell the TQQQ dips. — 16 replies — 37.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Once I get like a half a mill I'm just gonna buy and sell the TQQQ dips.

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:23 UTC

OP

Once I get like a half a mill I'm just gonna buy and sell the TQQQ dips. They happen fairly often

Early replies (first 3)

62156267
>>62156258
I bought SOXL for 9 dollars last year but had to sell it around 25 bucks to pay for emergency expenses.
Leveraged ETFs seem too good to be true, if I held that it wouldve been 10x in a year, it felt like a no brainer because the demand for semiconductors has been unmet for the last 5 years

62156274
if I had half a million I'd be all in on the next 100x.

62156279
>>62156274
I got 300k so I'm not that far off. Bitcoin going back to ath would really help

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156298
>>62156267
>Leveraged ETFs seem too good to be true
Hype for the next one to drop

62156312
>>62156258
just don't sell wenn it keeps dipping after your buy in. be patient and let it dip hard, don't sweat missing out on a dip

62156323
>>62156279
>300k
Are you the 40yo walmart guy who posted the other day about needing a 10x?

62156341
>>62156258
500K is already a lot of money. It's literally enough to retire, I hope you lose everything on your gambling, Faggot.

62156348
>>62156341
>enough to retire
Nigga please.

62156353
QQQ dropped 37% in 2021. You would have gone to 0. Promise you wouldn’t have survived that “buying the dips in TQQQ”, given the nature of 3x ETFs.

62156358
>>62156323
Lol no but I know who you're talking about

62156361
>>62156353
Just wait and you're fine

62156372
>>62156353
was 2022 actually

62156447
>>62156348
Unless you're a Jew because Jew can't ever retire from jewing.

62156589
>>62156361

90% down on 1000 is 100
90% up on 100 is 190
If you held through a -30% drawdown you’d need a 300% move in QQQ to break even

>>62156372

Peak to trough started 21

62156613
>>62156589
You'd sell here because you're low iq. You'd also be buying the peaks

62157976
>>62156613
You're a genius

item.title: QUANTUM COMPUTER BREAKS ECC KEY IN BITCOIN-RELATED TEST — 10 replies — 32.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
QUANTUM COMPUTER BREAKS ECC KEY IN BITCOIN-RELATED TEST

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:30 UTC

OP

>A researcher used public quantum hardware to break a 15-bit elliptic curve key, winning Project Eleven’s 1 BTC Q-Day Prize.

>The test does not threaten Bitcoin’s 256-bit security yet, but highlights growing urgency around post-quantum upgrades, with ~6.9M BTC sitting in addresses with exposed public keys.

Why is no one talking about this?

Early replies (first 3)

62156396
>>62156349
because its a pop sci plebbit headline
there are still no quantum computers, and there's a good chance there will never be

62156397
ONE
NOTHIN WRONG WITH ME

62156454
it was a brute force attack not using shor. So not interesting the same way using your laptop break 15 bit ecc is not interesting

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156485
>>62156454
>not using shor
Lol, I hate the QC headlines

62156501
>>62156396
What???? We are just lying now?

62156509
>>62156349
Nigger, you need to lurk bitcoin twitter more. It's being discussed all day.

62156518
>>62156501
They straight up just don't work. They are retarded expensive copper chandeliers in a cold room.

62157972
>>62156349
Fake and gay

62157980
>>62156349
kumar, stop it, you miss the 60k train but you can still sit on the roof of the next one, like you're used to

62157985
You can break a 15-bit encryption key on a TI-84 calculator.

item.title: STRC is literally a ponzi — 7 replies — 26.0/hr

STRC is literally a ponzi

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-26 07:32 UTC

Did you fall for it, anon?

62155371
i bought 1 share of msty as a joke, then in like 2 months it reverse split and it got liquidated because it was less than 1 shares worth lmao.

62156143
>>62155322

62156154
>>62155322
Isn't Schiff the child rapist?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156155
>>62155322
Schiff does the same thing just with shiny rocks

62157986
>>62156154
Yes

62157988
BOOMER ON BOOMER VIOLENCE

62157990
>>62155371
>bought 500 bucks as a joke
>lost 400 bucks
I want my money back
Also I realized you don't actually really benefit from 'tax writeoffs' on losses, like it doesn't change shit if you're making under 100k ingains and over 100k you'd be better of changing your filing status.

Never ever gonna buy 500 bucks on something as 'a joke' again.

item.title: >NW in 2021: $1.3M >NW in 2026: $550k — 25 replies — 25.9/hr

62151602
>>62151595

62151615
>>62151602

62151661
>>62151602
>>62151615

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62151668
>>62151661
So why didn't you invest in the shit and piss 500 and keep working?

62151688
>>62151661

62151738
>>62151668
i already told you?? everyone including me thought bitcoin was going to go to at least $100k
>>62151688
im already dead inside, don't worry

62151783
And you probably called everyone else an idiot buying into "the inflationary fiat scam". Kys

62154513
>>62151602
Unrealized dog coin gains

62156280
>>62151661
you are a fucking megatard.

62156337
>>62151661
1.3M in S&P and get you an almost guaranteed 100k a year gain. easily enough to retire in a low or even medium cost of living area.

62156425
>>62151595

62156430
>NW in 2021
20k
>NW in 2026
100k

62156432
what does one do in this situation? Personally I would live minimalist as possible and wait for the DXY to correct, then go all into bitcoin or something

62156496
>>62151595
NW Feb 2025: 260k
NW Apr 2026: 540k

62156498
>>62156432
0dte sandisk calls

62156669
>>62151661
how the fuck do you have this much captial and not understand fear and greed.

62156677
>>62151595
Crypto is never coming back. It's dead. You've lost your chance. World has moved on. AI. Robotics. Precious metals. Oil. Nuclear. Nanotech. Photonics.

Crypto has no purpose.

62156715
>>62151738
>everyone including me thought bitcoin was going to go to at least $100k
bitcoin did go to $100k, you're just dumb

62156724
>>62151595
>>62151661
>>62151738
If you had placed your money in SPY in 2021 instead:
Nominal Price Return: 75.56%

Annualized: 11.52%

Investment Grew To: $2,282,317.56

Nominal Total Return (with dividends reinvested): 88.81%

Annualized: 13.11%

Investment Grew To: $2,454,466.50

that's pretty close to being able to retire and live off the dividends from SPY alone money.

62156753
>>62156715
paper hands on that fella

62156765
>>62151595

NW in 2026 (accounting for inflation): 350K

62156790
>>62151595

62156856

fuck you

62157877
>>62156856

62157928
>>62156430

item.title: SHORT MEMORY STOCKS RIGHT FUCKING NOW NIGGERS!!!! — 30 replies — 22.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SHORT MEMORY STOCKS RIGHT FUCKING NOW NIGGERS!!!!

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-04-26 06:27 UTC

OP

YOU HAVE NO IDEA. THIS IS 10x WORSE THAN TURBOQUANT.

Early replies (first 3)

62150764
>>62150761
proof??

62150766
>>62150764
EAT IT. DROPPED 30 MINS AGO.
https://huggingface.co/collections/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4

62150776
>>62150766
omg

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62150779
>>62150766
Explain please

62150780
>>62150761
done. 10x leverage.

62150782
>>62150779
Uses 10% of the memory of other SOTA AI models while maintaining equivalent benchmarks. Every AI company is about to get assraped.

62150787
>>62150761
>>62150782
This same conundrum happened with Turboquant. Using less memory means we can do more with our memory. This is not an issue

62150789
i busted your moms sk hynix

62150790
>>62150787
Normies will think otherwise when the press gets hold of this. Also basic supply and demand. AI companies in the short term will have to play catch up. Datacenters won't be buying any RAM until then. I agree that this is bullish in the long term though.

62150798
>>62150782
this happens every weeks, model always ends up being shit and nothing happens..

62150799
>>62150766
NEW FUD JUST DROPPED

62150803
>>62150761
>https://huggingface.co/collections/deepseek-ai/deepseek-v4
This still needs shit tons of memory. 128GB minimum to locally host it.

62150804
>>62150790
The thing is that memory is necessary retardless, it means they can buy even more memory for even more complex calculations.

But I do agree that memory stocks will probably take a haircut in the short term. I only wish I was less of a ngga last year or the year before and went harder I won't fuck up this time bitches gonna drop six sigma figgos in at the lows.

62150811
>>62150782
What about bittensor (ticker: TAO)?

62150815
>>62150761
>shitty chink model means memory demand is down.
Why do we let yuropoors post again?

62150838
>>62150761
if it was true then btc would have crashed chuddie

62152006
GUYS? GUYS? GUYS? WE NEED TO SHORT MEMORY GUYS! WE NEED TO SHORT IT NOW!

Yeah I hope you got out with your 2%. This is definitely no Turboquant

62152234
I finna get me sum cheap computer parts

62152238
>>62150761
I have a bad memory!

62152335
Historically if you look at Chink nand, it doing well hasn't hurt people like SNDK.
Deepseek is more bullish for CXMT, because Huawei wants to source their hbm domestically.
What follows is that I don't think samsung/skhynix/micron end up hurt by any of this Chinese demand. It's more demand not less I dunno what OP is smoking.

62153018
>>62152335
Because the Chinks don't even attempt to undercut, they charge an even higher premium, if they ever actually sold at a discount.

62154589
>>62150761
Bleeding monthly with high electricity cost. No thanks.

62154617
>>62150782
>>62150798 This. It's just made up shit to attempt crashing share prices so they can slurp. Happens every time.

62156802
>>62154617
true

62156835
>>62150761
Memory stocks as in SNDK?

62156842
>>62150766
'Eat It' by Weird-Al Yankovic dropped in 1983.

The news has been priced in decades ago.

62156848
More memory efficient AI causes more ambitious AI projects which increases memory use.

That has been the reality for years now. But yes, there is a small chance for a small short term dip from this news.

62156860
>investment fund I’m heavy into recently sold everything it had in openai to become even more *squints* Klarna heavy
How fucked am I?

62156952
>>62150761

62157882
>>62156835

item.title: I hate poor people — 15 replies — 14.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I hate poor people

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-04-26 06:45 UTC

OP

As someone that grew up with a dirt poor family and had to learn money literacy growing up (now a milloinaire at 30, from 25) i genuinely fucking hate poor people.

As soon as I got money (got luck on ethereum, both at 8 dollars, sold at 850), my family wanted to cannibalize it right away to buy brand new car, gucci handbags, iphones etc. Legitimately, I was giving so them so much but it wasnt enough.

Thats when I realized whhy they were poor in the first place. They couldve lifted themselves out of poverty but they legitimately have brains like n*ggers and have to IMMEDIATELY spend the money instead of putting it into an ETF etc.

Im so fucking ashamed that I dont talk to my mom anymore but I know its for the best.

At some stage it got so bad that they were just treating me like a an ATM machine.

Im not a sadistic person, but I fucking take pleasure ethat they're living in severe poverty and its all their fault.

Being poor, first and foremost, is a sickness of the mind, before anything else.

Early replies (first 3)

62156128
>>62156120
Same I have cut contact with my entire family and I am much happier just doing what is best for me. They are worthless people and I am ashamed that I have their dna inside me.

62156129
>>62156120
>Brand new cars, iphones, Gucci handbags
How brown are you

62156159
>>62156120
Ok thats sexy and all, but when can i buy this shit under 1500?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62156228
>>62156120
So many indicators in your text that their low IQ has been fully passed on to you as well.

62156278
>>62156228
nigga im typing fast chill out

62156345
>>62156120
I used to be like this. it was due to 1) being tired of having nothing. It's easy to sit back and say "leave all you money in an ETF" when you're already comfortable. 2) I didn't know anything about ETFs. I thought the stock market was just gambling.

62156346
> bought at $8 and sold at $850
> They couldve lifted themselves out of poverty
Survivorship bias on full display here.
> BUT I WORKED HARD FOR EVERY LAST CENT OF THAT MONEY!
Move along everyone.

62156391
>>62156346
>> BUT I WORKED HARD FOR EVERY LAST CENT OF THAT MONEY!

Unironically I did. I worked hard for the few thousand bucks, that I then invested/speculated into an emerging asset.

Stay mad. Stay poor.

62156398
>>62156391
He is just mad that he missed the chance and now next chance is ai, well guess what he is missing that as well. You already know why poor people are poor.

62156406
is this a copypasta from 2017. How would you 'become a millionaire from 2021 to 2026' buying ethereum, something which has only 2xed from the 2022 lows? Did you already have 500k or something?

62156409
>>62156346
This is literally just a copypasta. Eth was 8 bucks..... in 2016. OP is posting a 10yo copypasta.

62156411
>>62156346
This.
>>62156391
If you had put it in gold or even a decent stock I'd somewhat agree with you but you literally gambled on crypto and got lucky.

62156434
>>62156406
no, i made a few hundred k from eth when i was 22, i became a millionaire at 25 after some investments

62156443
>>62156411
>If you had put it in gold or even a decent stock I'd somewhat agree with you but you literally gambled on crypto and got lucky.
cope.

62157920
>>62156120
Show hands

item.title: Remote Work Routes for Electrical Engineers — 14 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Remote Work Routes for Electrical Engineers

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-04-26 06:35 UTC

OP

People who work from home with an engineering degree, what do you do?

I'm graduating in a year and can't bring myself to deal with corporate BS after doing 2 internships, which both felt like hell specifically because of the corporate environment. I'd finish my projects weeks before they needed to be done and could feel my precious time being sapped away. Needing to "find more work" to do with no additional pay incentive is the most cucked thing ever and needs to be avoided at all costs. I'm sure most of you can relate to that.

Anyone with an engineering degree here that does freelance/remote work where they can spend a day max grinding out projects and get the rest of the week to themselves? Job stacking freelance type roles seems like the play for someone who gets shit done and doesn't suck off upper management from their wage cells all day.

Early replies (first 3)

62155809
>>62155791
Im a ECE with 3 years in industry. Been applying to remote stuff ~50 applications for the past year and havent gotten an interview. Market is a lot tougher for remote work then normal jobs and most of the remote positions are for more people with 8+ years of experiance. Its rough in our field since otherwise you would need to move far for work.

good luck

62155813
I know of an electrical engineer that offers consultations.

62155855
>>62155809
might I ask what you're currently doing? Im looking for a role where my time is valued. Getting paid for being another body in the office is my nightmare.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62155953
>>62155855
I do "Design for Test" work. Anytime you get a salaried position like i got your time isn't going to be valued by the employer. You need to value it yourself by not working overtime. If you're not willing to work overtime you'll need to be good at your job and even then you may need to every now and then. Depending on the company you can leave early some days when you get your stuff done.

100% aim to get a remote job, thats my goal but just because i dont want to live in a city.

62155999
An European EE here. Wfh most of the time. Pick an industry and grind in it until you become efficient to a point where they dont need to keep an eye on you. It took me something like 8-9 years. Maybe you can do it faster. I've started in design, moved on to compliance and technical assurance, then moved on to another sector. Now comfy. Good luck. You only get what you build yourself, noone will give you anything for free...

62156030
You can make.a vaporware company and get people to invest fully remote. Just make it about space or quantum so musktards will buy it with zero proof of concept.

62156216
>>62155791
kek thanks for reminding me about how fucking stupid zoomshits are. You are not getting a remote job with no real experience. You will learn some basic social skills and give up the zoomer stare

62156306
>>62156216
You're clearly a retard because you didn't even read my post. I have experience. I know enough about the industry to realize I can contribute more when I have control of my time and resources. You zogslaves think it's dignifying to sit in a wage cell for weeks on end acting busy when they could do their job in a few hours and live life.

Social skills are for cucked losers that think people care about them. kek kek kek ngmi

You will feed zog. You will like it. You will talk about the weather.

62156310
>>62156216
>You will learn some basic social skills and give up the zoomer stare

picrel

62156778
>>62156216
boomers would rather have a 3 hour meeting where nothing gets done

62156784
>>62156216
millennialcucks... do we really??

62156870
>>62156784
We don't, anon. We really don't. Zogslaves will be zogslaves at the end of the day. They're ngmi.

62157875
>>62155791
Switch to plumbing

62157895
>>62155791
>Anyone with an engineering degree here that does freelance/remote work where they can spend a day max grinding out projects and get the rest of the week to themselves?
So I know in my field (Civil) there are people who work independently who could feasibly have a schedule like that.
(It's part of the reason avg salary is somewhat low: very competitive field)
Be that as it may, I'm not sure how it works in EEng but in Civil you need to get that PE stamp before you can really do anything like that. Or maybe have some weird partnership with a PE but you might be ultimately screwing yourself doing that.
It is totally possible to do though. You will need an expensive couple-thousand-per-year AutoCAD subscription (or whatever you'd use) and there might be some professional insurance as well just in case you fuck up.

Tranny redd*tors cause more mental illness then maraijuana

Stupid goy.

>>62153098

62154587

you guys are coping hard

62156379
>>62152347

62157939

item.title: TICK TOCK — 12 replies — 7.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
TICK TOCK

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-04-26 06:15 UTC

OP

TICK TOCK

Early replies (first 3)

62153825
Who gives a fuck about crypto? Do you retards have any idea what the implications are here? You have government agencies storing ALL data transferred over the internet. That is soon to be unencrypted. Think of what this means for every government, business, and individuals having 100% of their private data exposed.

62153839
>>62153776
15 bits, ooooooo, you can brute force 15 bits on traditional hardware easy

62153852
>project eleven
it's a scam lol
they fed it the answer
this is probably one of the most pathetic quantum scams of the decade
extremely low effort

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62153853
>>62153839
In case anyone doesn't know, 15 bits = 2^15 = 32768 combinations. You could crack that with a handful of monkeys rolling dice.

62153858
Muh crypto is the least concern if ECC is broken.

62153863
you nigger scam is too easy to expose now that we have claude

62154465
>>62153776
wooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo
now do it with the 256 bit key
to give you fuckers an idea: if you tested trillions of value per seconds and that you spent the next trillion year testing keys non stop, you'd have effectively tested the equivalent of a rounding error of the total number of keys

62155302
>>62153863
>private key is known from the start to the attacker
>in fact it is a constant value compiled into the circuit
lol
LAMAO

62155350
>>62153776
Wow it's nothing

62155380
>>62153776
> 15 bit
Nigga Btc uses 256 bit keys.
Chess has been solved for 7 pieces or less on the board. Try 32 pieces.

62155387
don't care got ALGO

62157863
>>62153776
Sell your BTC SV
